name: Choucroute Garni
recipeInstructions:
1. Use mild Italian sausages, bratwurst, or kielbasa for the sausage portion.
2. Dice the bacon.
3. Rinse the sauerkraut in a strainer under cold water and drain well.
4. In a large frying pan, sauté the bacon with onions until the meat is browned.
5. Peel and dice the apples.
6. Place the sauerkraut and diced apples into the crockpot.
7. Arrange the pork chops and onions on top of the sauerkraut mixture.
8. Add the garlic, peppercorns, and white wine to the crockpot.
9. Cover and cook on low (200°F) for 4-6 hours.
10. Add the sausages to the crockpot and continue cooking for an additional hour.
Sylvia's comments: I used only half of the sauerkraut, which was sufficient. The canning liquid added a lovely flavor to the dish. No precooking of meat was necessary; it was layered in the crockpot before turning it on in the morning and served with noodles when ready. The servings were generous, making the recipe thoroughly satisfactory. In previous versions, brown sugar might have been included, which could enhance the flavor next time.
